I’m In Your Mind contained alternate lyrics. Before The Wholly Ghost, Stu asks Joey if they should play Angel next which was the working title for Her and I (Slow Jam 2) and Joey says they should play Ghost instead the working title for The Wholly Ghost. Before Her and I, Stu asks the crowd "Do you mind if we play a chilled out jammy one or is that boring?".  This is the earliest recording available for the song, which strongly resembles the studio version, although they are still working out the jam sections.  After, Stu asks if they can keep playing new stuff because "they need practice".  Following Am I in Heaven?, Stu shouts out the opener, Ice Balloons, and gives them a "big extra thank you" for letting them borrow their equipment.
opener: Blackstone Rngrs, Ice Balloons, Christmas Pets

showtitle: Panache CMJ Day Party & Hangover Brunch
shownotes: Stripped down set played by Stu, Joey, and Cavs. This show featured the debut of Stressin'. Following Her and I (Slow Jam 2), Joey mentioned that it was "too early for jazz", to which Cavs briefly teased The River. During It's Got Old, Ambrose played harmonica from the audience. Vegemite was announced as a song about Australia's national dish. During God is Calling Me Back Home, which was played for the first time known since 2013-12-31 (73 show gap), Stu was brought a piece of cake by Tim Presley from White Fence and Cate Le Bon, which Eric and the band's manager Michelle Cable then fed to Stu. “Happy Birthday to You” was sung to Stu at the end of the set.
tourname: 2014 I'm In Your Mind Fuzz - North America

shownotes: Head On/Pill featured Stu on flute. Near the end of Black Tooth, a fan fell onto a member of the band. Following Danger $$$, Cavs teased The River. Before the Mind Fuzz suite and during Cellophane, Cookie teased Good Times (Chic). Following the Mind Fuzz suite, Cookie teased God Is Calling Me Back Home while the band discussed what to play next. Before Hot Wax, which contained a Life Is Cool tease, the band dedicated the song to all their moms as well as their “tour mom”, Michelle Cable, who joined them on stage and played maracas.
